books.title: The Tale of Peter Rabbit
books.author: Beatrix Potter
books.blurb: Naughty Peter Rabbit sneaks into Mr. McGregor's garden — and almost doesn't make it home for tea. A timeless tale with beautiful watercolor pictures.

books.title: Owl at Home
books.author: Arnold Lobel
books.blurb: Owl invites Winter in for tea, talks to two big bumps under his blanket, and tries to find his upstairs and his downstairs at the same time. Gentle, funny chapter stories.

books.title: Frog and Toad Are Friends
books.author: Arnold Lobel
books.blurb: Frog and Toad do everything together — even when they disagree about almost everything. Five sweet stories about best friends.

books.title: Nate the Great
books.author: Marjorie Weinman Sharmat
books.blurb: Nate is a kid detective, and his friend Annie has lost a picture. Time to put on the detective hat — and have some pancakes.

books.title: Henry and Mudge: The First Book
books.author: Cynthia Rylant
books.blurb: Henry has no brothers and no sisters, so his parents get him a dog. Mudge grows from 7 pounds to 180 pounds — and becomes Henry's best friend.

books.title: The Dot
books.author: Peter H. Reynolds
books.blurb: Vashti is sure she can't draw. Her teacher says: 'Just make a mark and see where it takes you.' What starts as one tiny dot becomes something amazing.

books.title: Where the Wild Things Are
books.author: Maurice Sendak
books.blurb: Max is sent to bed without supper, and a forest grows in his room. He sails off to where the wild things are — and becomes their king.

books.title: The Story about Ping
books.author: Marjorie Flack
books.blurb: Ping is a duck who lives on a boat on the Yangtze River. One night he misses the boat home — and has a great adventure before finding his family again.

books.title: Corduroy
books.author: Don Freeman
books.blurb: Corduroy is a small teddy bear in a big department store. He's been waiting a long time for someone to take him home — and tonight he sets out to find his missing button.

books.title: Knuffle Bunny
books.author: Mo Willems
books.blurb: Trixie can't talk yet. So when she loses her favorite bunny at the laundromat, telling Daddy what's wrong is the hardest part. A super-funny first adventure.

books.title: The Ugly Duckling
books.author: Hans Christian Andersen
books.blurb: A little duckling doesn't look like the others. He travels through a long winter feeling all alone — until one spring day he discovers exactly who he is.

books.title: The True Story of the Three Little Pigs
books.author: Jon Scieszka
books.blurb: You think you know the story? Well, the wolf says everyone got it wrong. He just had a cold, and was borrowing a cup of sugar. A hilarious retelling.

books.title: Owl Moon
books.author: Jane Yolen
books.blurb: Late one winter night, a girl and her father walk through the snowy woods to look for an owl. A quiet, beautiful book that whispers.

books.title: The Velveteen Rabbit
books.author: Margery Williams
books.blurb: A toy rabbit longs to be Real. With the love of one little boy and some skin-horse wisdom, he learns what really makes something real.

books.title: The Lighthouse Family: The Storm
books.author: Cynthia Rylant
books.blurb: A cat, a dog, and three little mice live in a lighthouse on the sea. When a storm rolls in, they help a stranded sailor — and a family is born. Series book 1.

books.title: Flat Stanley: His Original Adventure
books.author: Jeff Brown
books.blurb: When a bulletin board falls on Stanley, he wakes up flat as a pancake — only half an inch thick. Now he can slide under doors, fly like a kite, and even be mailed in an envelope.

books.title: Mercy Watson to the Rescue
books.author: Kate DiCamillo
books.blurb: Mercy is a pig who LOVES buttered toast. One night she crawls into her humans' bed, the bed collapses, and a chaotic 'rescue' begins.

books.title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
books.author: Roald Dahl
books.blurb: Three nasty farmers are out to catch Mr. Fox. But Mr. Fox is faster, smarter, and has a brilliant plan. A short, thrilling Roald Dahl classic.

books.title: Stellaluna
books.author: Janell Cannon
books.blurb: A baby fruit bat falls into a bird's nest and is raised as a bird — until one starry night she discovers who she really is. A heartwarming story about being different and finding family.

books.title: The Magic Faraway Tree
books.author: Enid Blyton
books.blurb: At the top of a magical tree, a different fantastic land arrives every day. The kids climb up to find Birthday Land, the Land of Topsy-Turvy, the Land of Sweets…

books.title: Magic Tree House #1: Dinosaurs Before Dark
books.author: Mary Pope Osborne
books.blurb: Jack and Annie find a tree house full of books. Open one and POOF — they're in a different time. Their first adventure: back to the age of the dinosaurs.

books.title: A to Z Mysteries: The Absent Author
books.author: Ron Roy
books.blurb: Dink's favorite author was supposed to come to the bookstore — but he never shows up! Dink and his friends Josh and Ruth Rose follow the clues to find him.

books.title: Junie B. Jones and the Stupid Smelly Bus
books.author: Barbara Park
books.blurb: Junie B. is starting kindergarten and is NOT — repeat, NOT — getting on the smelly bus to come home. She has a hilarious plan instead.

books.title: The Boxcar Children
books.author: Gertrude Chandler Warner
books.blurb: Four orphans — Henry, Jessie, Violet, and Benny — find an old boxcar in the woods and make it their home. Can they take care of each other?

books.title: Charlotte's Web
books.author: E.B. White
books.blurb: A clever spider named Charlotte writes words in her web to save a friendly pig named Wilbur. A tender story of friendship, life, and goodbye.

books.title: Stuart Little
books.author: E.B. White
books.blurb: Stuart Little is a mouse born to a human family in New York City. He sails toy boats in Central Park, drives a tiny car, and sets off to find his lost friend.

books.title: Because of Winn-Dixie
books.author: Kate DiCamillo
books.blurb: Opal finds a big stray dog in the supermarket and names him Winn-Dixie. He helps her make friends and even helps her understand her family.

books.title: Geronimo Stilton: Lost Treasure of the Emerald Eye
books.author: Geronimo Stilton
books.blurb: Geronimo is a journalist mouse who doesn't really like adventure — but his cousin Trap drags him on a treasure hunt across a tropical island.

books.title: We Are in a Book!
books.author: Mo Willems
books.blurb: Gerald the elephant and Piggie discover something amazing — they are characters in a BOOK! Will they say the magic word before the story ends?

books.title: I Will Surprise My Friend!
books.author: Mo Willems
books.blurb: Piggie wants to surprise Gerald — but Gerald has the same idea! They hide and hide and hide. Will they ever find each other?

books.title: Are You Ready to Play Outside?
books.author: Mo Willems
books.blurb: Piggie can't WAIT to play outside! Then it starts to rain. Can Gerald and Piggie find a way to have fun anyway?

books.title: There Is a Bird on Your Head!
books.author: Mo Willems
books.blurb: Gerald has a bird on his head. Then TWO birds. Then a nest. Then eggs. Then a whole bird family! What's an elephant to do?

books.title: Should I Share My Ice Cream?
books.author: Mo Willems
books.blurb: Gerald has a yummy ice cream. Should he share it with Piggie? He thinks. And thinks. And thinks…

books.title: Hop on Pop
books.author: Dr. Seuss
books.blurb: Dr. Seuss's simplest book ever — short rhymes with words you already know. We like to hop. We like to hop on Pop!

books.title: One Fish Two Fish Red Fish Blue Fish
books.author: Dr. Seuss
books.blurb: Walking, talking, biking, hiking — meet a wild bunch of creatures in this silly rhyming romp from Dr. Seuss.

books.title: Biscuit
books.author: Alyssa Satin Capucilli
books.blurb: Biscuit the little yellow puppy just won't go to bed. He wants a snack. He wants water. He wants a hug. What WILL it take?

books.title: Little Bear
books.author: Else Holmelund Minarik
books.blurb: Four cozy stories about a little bear and his mother — a snowsuit, a birthday wish, a trip to the moon (sort of), and a goodnight kiss.

books.title: Frog and Toad All Year
books.author: Arnold Lobel
books.blurb: Frog and Toad share five gentle stories through the seasons — sledding, ice cream, a Christmas surprise, and the joy of being friends all year long.

books.title: Goose on the Loose
books.author: Phil Roxbee Cox (Usborne)
books.blurb: A goose is on the loose! Will the boy with the broom be able to catch her in time? A short rhyming chase through the farm.

books.title: Pirate Pat
books.author: Mairi Mackinnon (Usborne)
books.blurb: Pirate Pat sails his little boat across the sea looking for treasure. A short, rhyming Usborne reader with a parrot, a whale, and an island full of gold.

books.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar
books.author: Eric Carle
books.blurb: In the light of the moon, a little egg lay on a leaf. The caterpillar that hatches is very, very hungry — and what he eats next is delicious.

books.title: The Cat in the Hat
books.author: Dr. Seuss
books.blurb: On a cold, wet day, Sally and her brother are stuck inside — until a tall cat in a red striped hat arrives and turns the whole house upside down.

books.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
books.author: Michael Rosen
books.blurb: Swishy swashy! Splash splosh! A family stomps, splashes, and squelches through long grass, a river, and mud to find a bear.

books.title: Goldilocks and the Three Bears
books.author: James Marshall
books.blurb: A nosy little girl wanders into a bear family's house and tries every chair, every bowl, and every bed. The bears come home with quite a surprise.

books.title: Mother Goose's Nursery Rhymes
books.author: Iona Opie (ed.)
books.blurb: A classic collection of rhymes and verses — from Humpty Dumpty to Jack and Jill — perfect for reading aloud, singing, and learning by heart.

books.title: The Gruffalo
books.author: Julia Donaldson
books.blurb: A clever little mouse takes a walk through the deep dark wood and meets a fox, an owl, and a snake — and then something far scarier. Or is it?

books.title: If You Give a Mouse a Cookie
books.author: Laura Numeroff
books.blurb: Give a mouse a cookie, and he'll ask for a glass of milk. Then a straw. Then a napkin. A funny chain of one-thing-leads-to-another.

books.title: Green Eggs and Ham
books.author: Dr. Seuss
books.blurb: Sam-I-am will not stop offering green eggs and ham. Not in a house, not with a mouse — but maybe, just maybe, one tiny taste?

books.title: The Enormous Turnip
books.author: Traditional (Usborne)
books.blurb: An old man plants a turnip seed. It grows enormous — so big that everyone has to pull together to get it out. How many helpers will it take?

books.title: The Gingerbread Man
books.author: Traditional (Usborne)
books.blurb: Run, run, as fast as you can! A freshly baked gingerbread man jumps out of the oven and runs from everyone who tries to catch and eat him.

books.title: Chicken Licken
books.author: Traditional (Usborne)
books.blurb: An acorn falls on Chicken Licken's head — and she's sure the sky is falling! She gathers her friends to warn the king. But a sly fox has other ideas.

books.title: Jack and the Beanstalk
books.author: Traditional (Usborne)
books.blurb: Jack trades their cow for magic beans — and overnight a beanstalk grows into the clouds. At the top is a giant's castle full of treasure. And a very large, angry giant.

books.title: The Princess and the Pea
books.author: Hans Christian Andersen, illus. Dorothée Duntze
books.blurb: A prince searches the world for a real princess. One stormy night a visitor arrives — but can the queen's clever test really tell if she's genuine?

books.title: The Elves and the Shoemaker
books.author: Brothers Grimm (Usborne)
books.blurb: A kind shoemaker is too poor to buy leather. Each night a mystery helper secretly finishes the shoes — and business has never been better. Who is it?

books.title: Rapunzel
books.author: Susanna Davidson (Usborne)
books.blurb: A baby girl with long golden hair is locked in a tall tower by a wicked witch. One day a prince hears her singing — and a clever escape begins.

books.title: Pinocchio
books.author: Carlo Collodi (Usborne)
books.blurb: Geppetto carves a wooden puppet who can talk and walk by himself. Pinocchio wants to be a real boy — but first he has to learn what it means to be good.
